findings.title: Uppercase URL in import-sub treated as file path
findings.suggested_fix: Use case-insensitive comparison for URL check: convert importFilePath to lowercase before checking, or use a case-insensitive string comparison function.
evidence: This only matches lowercase 'http://' and 'https://' at position 0
'HTTP://example.com' does not start with 'http://', so rfind returns npos
isUrl becomes false, code treats it as a file path
std::filesystem::exists('HTTP://example.com') returns false (not a valid file path)
Import fails unexpectedly for valid uppercase URLs

findings.title: Multiple 'log_' prefixes in log level string incorrectly parsed
findings.suggested_fix: Use a loop to remove all 'log_' prefixes, or check for the prefix after the first removal and remove again if present.
evidence: No loop, so second 'log_' prefix is not removed

findings.title: Whitespace in log level string causes incorrect parsing
findings.suggested_fix: Trim whitespace from the input string before processing, or use a trim function in stringToLevel().
evidence: Trigger: Config has 'file_level': ' ERROR ' (with leading/trailing spaces)
Check: s.size() > 4 → true (7 characters with spaces)
All other checks fail due to spaces

findings.title: xray_api_port not validated for valid range
findings.suggested_fix: Add validation: if config.xray_api_port <= 0, set to a default value (e.g., 10080).
evidence: No validation for positive value or valid port range
If api_port is -1 or 0, xray fails to bind to the port
Proxy testing fails because xray is not running

findings.title: No upper bound validation for xray_workers
findings.suggested_fix: Add upper bound validation: if config.xray_workers > MAX_WORKERS (e.g., 100), set to MAX_WORKERS or a reasonable default.
evidence: Validation: if config.xray_workers <= 0 → set to 1 (only checks lower bound)

findings.title: Potential integer overflow in config value parsing
findings.suggested_fix: Check if the int64_t value fits in int before casting, or use int64_t for config values and validate range.
evidence: Trigger: Config has 'workers': 4294967296 (2^32, exceeds 32-bit int range)
4294967296 as int64_t, cast to int (32-bit on Windows)
Result might be 0 or negative (if wrapped)
If result is 0 or negative, validation sets it to 1
If result is positive but unexpected, workers might behave incorrectly

findings.title: import-sub with directory path passes exists() check but may fail unexpectedly
findings.suggested_fix: Add a check: if isUrl is false, verify that the path is a regular file (not a directory) using std::filesystem::is_regular_file().
evidence: Function might fail, return false, or behave unexpectedly
Import fails with unclear error message

residual_risks: Logger thread safety: The logging function (not shown in diff) might not have proper mutex locking for fileLevel_/consoleLevel_ reads, leading to race conditions if called from multiple threads simultaneously.
Config file path traversal: If the config has relative paths with '..', the behavior depends on resolvePath() implementation which is not shown in the diff.
SQLite concurrent access: Multiple processes accessing the same SQLite database might cause issues despite WAL mode and busy timeout configuration.
Logger::init() failure not handled: The code does not check if Logger::init() succeeds. If it fails (e.g., cannot create log directory), subsequent Logger calls might crash or behave unexpectedly.
Curl global cleanup order: In some error paths, curl_global_cleanup() is called before Logger::close(), but if Logger uses curl (unlikely), this could cause issues.
testing_gaps: Test stringToLevel() with edge cases: whitespace (' ERROR '), multiple prefixes ('LOG_LOG_ERROR'), mixed case ('Log_Error'), empty string.
Test import-sub command with uppercase URLs ('HTTP://example.com', 'HTTPS://example.com'), directory paths ('.', '..'), non-existent files.
Test config validation with boundary values: workers = 0, -1, 1000000; start_port = 0, -1, 65536; api_port = 0, -1, 99999; timeout_ms = 0, -1, INT_MAX.
Test the refactored import-sub command with both URL and file paths to ensure the unification didn't introduce regressions in error handling or resource cleanup.
Test Logger initialization failure scenario: simulate Logger::init() failure and verify that the program handles it gracefully without crashing.
